explicit_text: When I entered the service, I was living in Chicago. Before being drafted, I worked there for the Crane Company assembling valves for ships. Chicago was my home base before I was inducted into the army at Camp Grant, Illinois.

explicit_text: I was inducted into the army at Camp Grant, Illinois. After induction, I was sent home for a two-week furlough before heading to basic training. Camp Grant was where my military journey officially began.

explicit_text: After my furlough, I went to Camp Walters, Texas, for basic training. The training there was tough but effective—they made a man out of me with calisthenics, hand-to-hand combat, rifle range, and long forced marches. I gained weight and strength during those weeks at Camp Walters.

explicit_text: Fairfield, California, was the debarkation point where we boarded the USS Bliss to sail across the Pacific Ocean. After finishing basic training and shipping out from Jacksonville, Florida, I arrived in Fairfield to embark on the troop transport ship. It was the last stop in the States before heading overseas.

explicit_text: I sailed on the USS Bliss from Fairfield, California, across the Pacific Ocean to New Guinea. The ship was designed as a troop transport, and I volunteered to work in the butcher shop below decks, which was a good place to be since many were seasick above. I later heard the USS Bliss was sunk in the European theater, but all troops had disembarked by then.

explicit_text: We landed at Buna Mission in New Guinea around early June 1944, shortly after the area had been secured. My platoon was assigned guard duty by a river north of Buna, and I was attached to the 32nd Infantry Division to replace losses from the Battle of Buna–Gona. I stayed there about a month before moving on with the division to other battles along the New Guinea coast.

explicit_text: I was attached to the 32nd Infantry Division, also known as the Red Arrow Division, after landing at Buna. The division got its name from World War I, symbolizing piercing enemy lines. We fought in New Guinea and moved through places like Aitape, Saidor, Finchhaven, Hollandia, and Morotai. I was part of the 126th Infantry Regiment within the division and volunteered for reconnaissance patrols behind enemy lines.
